name: Breadsticks (Plain or Seeded)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups All-purpose flour, or as needed
4 teaspoons Fennel or caraway seeds OR
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the warm water, sugar, and yeast in a small bowl and let work until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. In a large bowl of electric mixer, combine the yeast mixture, salt, oil, and 1-1/4 cups flour. Beat on medium speed for 4 to 5 minutes. If using an electric mixer, switch to dough hook and on low speed, continue to add the remaining flour as needed to make a medium stiff dough. Increase speed to medium and continue mixing until the dough cleans the bowl completely.
3. Turn the dough into a greased bowl and flip so that the greased side is up. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rise until doubled in bulk.
4. When proofing is complete, punch down the dough and knead in seeds if desired. Cover the dough again with plastic wrap and let rest for 10 to 15 minutes.
5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured working surface and divide into 32 pieces. Lay the plastic over the top.
6. Roll each piece one at a time to form a rope about 7 or 8 inches long (they'll be very thin). Place on lightly greased baking sheets and proof once more until doubled.
7. Preheat the oven to 325 F.
8. Bake in the center of the oven for the shelf until the breadsticks are nicely browned all over, taking care not to dry out during baking. After 5 minutes, spray with water using a trigger-type sprayer or place ice cubes in the bottom of the oven. Repeat after another 5 minutes, then complete baking.
9. Cool on racks and store airtight. Will keep for weeks.
